item.title: Mistral CEO: AI companies should pay a content levy in Europe
item.description: A revenue-based charge would protect the livelihoods of copyright holders and bring legal certainty

item.title: Is it time for me to buy equities again?
item.description: Many price/earnings ratios have fallen back to long-run averages

item.title: Can Jane Austen help my teenager?
item.description: The writer’s Regency settings hold lessons for making friends on social media

item.title: Interest rates take fright
item.description: The market’s calm in the face of war is beginning to crack

item.title: The case for slicing up consumer conglomerates further
item.description: Investors are better able to value pure plays, while lacklustre units can dilute overall growth and margins at sprawling companies

item.title: Quiet rituals of survival in Tehran
item.description: War feels like something that should have been left behind in a more brutal century

item.title: The war over tail risks is in full swing
item.description: Trump’s financial fight with insurers will affect business confidence as much as the kinetic battle

item.title: Maritime security is key to global energy supplies
item.description: Companies need to know their vessels can pass through busy shipping lanes repeatedly and safely

item.title: Musks remain at the top table of first-class roses
item.description: The centenary of the great rosarian Joseph Pemberton’s death is a beautiful excuse to revisit these hardy, floriferous and intensely scented varieties

item.title: This gas crisis is bad — but not as bad as the last one
item.description: Whatever happens in the Middle East, European prices are unlikely to rise as much as they did in 2022

item.title: Investors’ important bet on nuclear fusion
item.description: The elusive power source is gradually moving closer to becoming a reality

item.title: Europe needs to prepare now for an extended energy shock
item.description: Flows of oil and gas do not resume like turning a tap back on

item.title: War between Afghanistan and Pakistan has much wider consequences
item.description: When these countries become arenas for militancy, the repercussions reverberate far beyond their borders

item.title: Tokens may soon drive the AI economy
item.description: Jensen Huang of Nvidia has outlined a future based around the production, consumption and monetisation of output units

item.title: The UK must accept it is no longer a global power
item.description: Starmer has failed to level with the public that Britain’s control over its destiny is limited

item.title: Being stranded abroad sold me on AI travel agents
item.description: Some users may be reluctant to increase their dependence on Big Tech companies

item.title: Trustpilot deserves a more favourable appraisal
item.description: The online review platform floated in 2021 and now trades below its debut price

item.title: The dynamic globalisation machine will overcome the Iran shock
item.description: Short-term crisis management in shipping should not be mistaken for fatal weakness in trade

item.title: How (not) to help with high energy prices
item.description: Heed the lessons from 2022

item.title: Trump’s wars are made for TV
item.description: And, tragically, the real life death toll seems irrelevant

item.title: The Fed can only wait and wonder
item.description: Plus, the stock market’s wartime reversal

item.title: Spain is a role model in weathering Iranian oil shocks
item.description: The country’s speedy rollout of renewables has put a lid on electricity bills

item.title: Why bank cards may be heading the way of the cheque book
item.description: The £100 cap on contactless card spending is lifted today — but digital wallets matter more for future growth

item.title: Trump sails into battle with Lloyd’s of London
item.description: The market’s historic strength in marine war insurance is being challenged by the US

item.title: Economists have caught the AI bug
item.description: It’s expanding the scope of research but challenging the profession’s process of weeding out mistakes
